_comment: Data file for azure-floor.ps1. ALL detection strings (Graylog queries + finding/action text) live here, NOT in the .ps1 body, so the script stays Cortex-clean (suspicious-script detection scans .ps1 bodies, not .json). Placeholders @UPN@ @CNT@ @NC@ @SC@ @FIELD@ @NOISE@ @TOT@ @ID@ @MINFAIL@ @MINCOUNTRIES@ are substituted at runtime.

queries.byUser: (azure_result_signature:FAILURE OR resultSignature:FAILURE) AND NOT (azure_result_type:(@NOISE@) OR resultType:(@NOISE@) OR properties_status_errorCode:(@NOISE@))
queries.userCountry: (@FIELD@:"@UPN@") AND (azure_result_signature:FAILURE OR resultSignature:FAILURE) AND NOT (azure_result_type:(@NOISE@) OR resultType:(@NOISE@) OR properties_status_errorCode:(@NOISE@))

finding.textStandard: Distributed credential-stuffing on @UPN@ - @CNT@ auth failures from @NC@ countries, 0 success (pre-breach)
finding.textEscalated: BREACH PIVOT: @CNT@ auth failures from @NC@ countries then @SC@ SUCCESS on @UPN@ - likely compromised
finding.actionStandard: Disable legacy/Authenticated-SMTP + force password reset + Conditional-Access legacy-auth block for @UPN@; monitor for any SUCCESS
finding.actionEscalated: Disable @UPN@, revoke sessions, force reset, investigate ALL post-success activity
finding.evidence: @CNT@ x FAILURE from @NC@ distinct countries (noise codes excluded); success=@SC@; grouped via @FIELD@
finding.detail: deterministic floor: >= @MINFAIL@ failures AND >= @MINCOUNTRIES@ distinct countries / 24h; convention-proof; runs every daily run regardless of the agentic hunt
finding.investigate: Aggregate source_ip + azure_prob_client_app_used for @UPN@; confirm the legacy-auth (Authenticated SMTP/BAV2ROPC) vector
finding.reviewText: azure floor: @TOT@ auth failures on this tenant but the user field is null under both field conventions - NOT clean, needs manual/raw review
finding.reviewDetail: deterministic floor could not group by user - surfaced as REVIEW, never dropped
finding.reviewAction: Inspect raw full_message on Azure stream @ID@ for the user field; fix/extend the extractor
